title: Setting Up Your First Kosher Kitchen

sources.title: Leviticus Chapter 11
sources.description: Biblical source on kosher and non-kosher animals, separation of pure and impure

sources.title: Deuteronomy Chapter 14
sources.description: Biblical reiteration of kashrut laws including prohibition of meat and milk

sources.title: Your Kosher Kitchen
sources.description: Comprehensive guide to setting up and maintaining a kosher kitchen

sources.title: Koshering Your Kitchen
sources.description: Practical step-by-step instructions for making a kitchen kosher

sources.title: What is Kosher Food?
sources.description: Overview of kashrut laws and kosher kitchen requirements

key_concepts: Two sets of dishes, utensils, pots

chinese_parallels.parallel: Daoist concepts of purity (qing/清) and contamination (hui/秽)
chinese_parallels.note: Both traditions recognize categories of pure and impure that affect spiritual practice

chinese_parallels.parallel: Confucian emphasis on li (礼) - proper ordering and ritual correctness in daily life
chinese_parallels.note: Kosher kitchen requires same attention to detail and proper procedure as traditional Chinese ritual practices

chinese_parallels.parallel: Buddhist vegetarian kitchen practices and ritual food preparation
chinese_parallels.note: Both systems elevate eating from mere sustenance to spiritual discipline
